channel.title: Blog on Kashif Nazir
channel.description: Recent content in Blog on Kashif Nazir

item.title: Sora Broke My Deprecation Reflex
item.description: Sora's shutdown was a reminder that SaaS deprecation dates do not always behave like enterprise software timelines.

item.title: Automation Keeps Hitting the Same Wall
item.description: Zapier, RPA, and AI agents keep running into the same contract gap between orchestration tools and applications that were not built to be automated cleanly.

item.title: A DR Plan Is a Hypothesis You've Never Tested
item.description: A failed backup story about why disaster recovery plans, fault injection, and restore tests only matter once someone has proved the recovery path works.

item.title: A Week on a Three-Week-Old Keyboard
item.description: A week with Yaps showed where on-device voice typing works, where the real world gets in the way, and how quickly a three-week-old keyboard can improve.

item.title: Back on Windows With Mac Habits
item.description: A week back on an old Windows laptop showed what Windows 11 still gets right, what still gets in the way, and how much macOS muscle memory has started to stick.

item.title: The Service Account Problem Has a New Name
item.description: AI agent identity is being treated as a new governance problem, but most enterprises are still carrying the same unresolved service-account hygiene underneath it.

item.title: What Two AIs Saw in My Study
item.description: Asking ChatGPT and Claude the same vision-led room question showed a difference that benchmarks do not always catch: what each model thinks the answer is for.

item.title: What I Stopped Fighting on the SA Pro Recert
item.description: Moving AWS recert notes from OneNote to Notion, using Claude projects for dense study notes, and where the setup paid off on SA Pro weak spots.

item.title: Better Than What It Replaces
item.description: Self-service, automation, and AI testing only matter when they make the real task faster or easier for the person using them.

item.title: Everyone Wants a Good Handover. Nobody Wants to Write One.
item.description: Good documentation feels like overhead until the day it saves the incident, the handover, or your future self from starting again with no context.

item.title: Microsoft's New Outlook Took Two Years to Get Colour Categories Back
item.description: A small missing calendar rule in new Outlook shows the hidden cost of migrating users before the new client can carry their workflows.

item.title: The Ladder Didn't Break When AI Arrived
item.description: AI is eating some junior work, but the route into architecture had already been narrowed by cloud, specialisation, and the loss of accidental breadth.

item.title: AWS Summit London 2026 — Post-Summit
item.description: The AI Builders Breakfast ended up being the most useful part of the day, and it sharpened the same question I arrived with: where the application layer fits in the AWS Summit conversation.

item.title: AWS Summit London 2026 — Pre-Summit
item.description: I've never been to an AWS Summit before. On Wednesday I'm heading to ExCeL London, and I've already had to make some hard choices about how the day runs.

item.title: Why I Couldn't Containerise My Home Server
item.description: A 2013-era i5 meets Docker, WSL, and the reality that hardware ceilings only show up when you try something new.

item.title: Watching AI Learn to Play Red Light, Green Light
item.description: Trying AI Warehouse's Red Light, Green Light simulator and watching reinforcement learning agents shift from chaos to strategy.

item.title: Pilot to Production: The Gap Nobody Plans For
item.description: Pilots get support, rollouts get a prayer. Why OS migrations and AI tool rollouts fail in the same human ways.

item.title: AI for AWS Certs
item.description: I built two custom GPTs to help study for the AWS AI Practitioner exam. The note-creation one ate more time than it saved. The coaching one was worth keeping.

item.title: The Architecture Unbuilt
item.description: There are a lot of ways to build a personal site. I went with the simplest one. Here's what I considered, what I'm planning to build later, and some stuff about AI agents that I didn't expect to care about.

item.title: Building This Site
item.description: How I built kashifnazir.com in two weeks using Hugo, two AI agents, and zero web development experience.
